item.title: About
item.description:
Hi, I’m Naren

I’m a Cloud Architect with 15+ years of experience in software engineering, currently focused on building AI-ready cloud infrastructure and helping engineering teams scale with confidence.

This site is where I share:

  • Practical cloud architecture patterns I’ve used in production
  • AI systems design — from RAG pipelines to MLOps
  • Hands-on demos with real code and working examples
  • Lessons from the field — what worked, what didn’t, and why

What I Work With

  • AWS · GCP · Azure
  • Kubernetes · Terraform · Helm
  • Python · Go · distributed systems
  • LLMs · vector databases · AI pipelines

Why CloudToAILearn?

The cloud is no longer just infrastructure — it’s the foundation for every AI system being built today. I started this blog to bridge the gap between cloud architecture and AI engineering, with a focus on things that actually work in production.

item.title: From Cloud to Cognition: Architecting AI-Ready Infrastructure
item.description:
After 15 years of building cloud infrastructure, I’ve watched the industry shift from bare metal to VMs, VMs to containers, and containers to serverless. Each wave changed how we think about architecture.

AI is the next wave — and it’s different from all the others.

This post is about what that shift means for cloud architects, and the concrete changes you need to make to your infrastructure thinking today.

Why AI Workloads Are Different

Traditional cloud workloads are deterministic. You deploy an API, it handles requests, it returns predictable responses. You scale it horizontally, you monitor latency and error rates, you’re done.
